reasoning_steps: Step 1: IDENTIFY EXISTING COLORS - The guess has 5 distinct colors: red, blue, green, yellow, white. Feedback says pegs=1, meaning exactly one of these colors exists in the secret (at least once). The other four are not in the secret at all because if any other color from the guess were present, pegs would be ≥2.
Step 2: IDENTIFY LOCKED POSITIONS - Feedback says positions=0, so no color is in its guessed position. No locked positions.
Step 3: IDENTIFY MISPLACED COLORS - misplaced = pegs - positions = 1 - 0 = 1. The one existing color is misplaced: it is in the secret but not at its guessed position.

Step 4: IDENTIFY IMPOSSIBLE COLORS - We cannot yet identify which specific color is impossible because we don't know which one is the existing color. However, we know that four of the five guessed colors are definitely absent, but they remain unidentified. No specific color can be marked as impossible from this round alone.

Step 5: IDENTIFY UNKNOWNS - The secret has 5 positions. One position will hold the existing color (not its guessed position), and the other 4 positions need to be filled with colors from the full palette (which may include duplicates, but none of the four absent guessed colors). The existing color could appear multiple times (since only one occurrence matched in guess), so duplicates are possible. We know nothing else.

From prior round, exactly one of {red, blue, green, yellow, white} is present, and red/blue are now impossible (see step 4). Step 2: IDENTIFY LOCKED POSITIONS - positions=1 means exactly one guess color is in its exact position. The guessed positions are: green at 0, yellow at 1, white at 2, black at 3, purple at 4. The locked color could be the one from {green, yellow, white} if it occupies its guessed position, or black at 3, or purple at 4. Without further info, we cannot determine which.

Step 3: IDENTIFY MISPLACED COLORS - misplaced = pegs - positions = 3 - 1 = 2. The two present colors that are not locked are misplaced. So if the locked color is the one from {green, yellow, white}, then black and purple are misplaced; if locked is black, then the one from {green, yellow, white} and purple are misplaced; if locked is purple, then the one from {green, yellow, white} and black are misplaced.

Step 4: IDENTIFY IMPOSSIBLE COLORS - From prior round, exactly one of {red, blue, green, yellow, white} is present. To have pegs=3 from this guess (which includes green, yellow, white, black, purple), the only way is that the one present is from {green, yellow, white} and black and purple are present. Therefore red and blue are definitely absent. Additionally, two of {green, yellow, white} are absent (the ones not the present one), but we cannot identify which specific two yet.

Step 5: IDENTIFY UNKNOWNS - We still need to determine which of green, yellow, or white is present. Also need to find the exact locked position and the positions of the two misplaced colors. The secret has 5 positions; we have 3 confirmed present colors (black, purple, and one of the three). The remaining 2 positions are filled with unknown colors (could be duplicates of present colors or completely new colors). No information about other colors (e.g., orange, pink) exists yet.
